title: 米津玄師 - 海の幽霊 Kenshi Yonezu - Spirits of the Sea

songs.id: Kenshi Yonezu - Spirits of the Sea
songs.title: 米津玄師 - 海の幽霊 Kenshi Yonezu - Spirits of the Sea
songs.lyrics:
"Spirits of the Sea (海の幽霊)"
(by Kenshi Yonezu 米津玄師)
(from "Children of the Sea (海獣の子供)" soundtrack)

[Romanized:]

Akehanatareta
Kono heya ni wa dare mo inai
Shiokaze no nioi
Nijimitsuita isu ga hitotsu

Anata ga mayowanai you ni
Akete oku yo
Kishimu to o tataite
Nani kara hanaseba ii no ka
Wakaranaku naru kana

Hoshi ga furu yoru ni anata ni aeta
Ano yoru o wasure wa shinai
Taisetsu na koto wa kotoba ni naranai
Natsu no hi ni okita subete
Omoigakezu hikaru no wa
Umi no yuurei

Udaruru natsu no yuu ni
Kozue ga fune o miokuru
Ikutsuka no uta o sasayaku
Hana o chirashite

Anata ga dokoka de warau
Koe ga kikoeru
Atsui hoho no tezawari
Nejireta michi o susundara
Sono mabuta ga hiraku

Hanare banare demo tokimeku mono
Sakebou ima wa shiawase to
Taisetsu na koto wa kotoba ni naranai
Haneru hikari ni tokashite

Hoshi ga furu yoru ni anata ni aeta
Ano toki o wasure wa shinai
Taisetsu na koto wa kotoba ni naranai
Natsu no hi ni okita subete
Omoigakezu hikaru no wa
Umi no yuurei

Kaze kaoru sunahama de
Mata aimashou

[English Interpretation:]

The door is open,
no one’s inside.
Just a chair
that smells of the ocean breeze.

Its scent will guide you,
so I’ll keep it waiting for you.
Push open the creaking door—
I wonder where to start
when we speak again.

We met the night the stars fell,
a memory I’ll never forget.
The most important things need no words,
all that happened on those summer days
shines unexpectedly—
like spirits of the sea.

In the scorching summer evening,
the treetops watch the boats depart,
whispering quiet songs
and scattering petals.

Somewhere out there,
I can hear your laugh.
I remember the warmth of your cheeks.
Once you pass that winding road,
your eyes will open.

Even when we’re far apart,
our hearts still tremble for each other.
Let’s shout that this is happiness.
The most important things need no words—
let them melt into the light.

We met the night the stars fell,
and I’ll never forget that moment.
The most important things need no words,
everything from those summer days
shines unexpectedly—
like spirits of the sea.

Perhaps we’ll meet again
on the shore where the sea breeze blows.
